center.name: 约翰·莱瑟姆 (鸟类学家)
center.summary: 约翰·莱瑟姆 (鸟类学家)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联50种鸟类，包括红耳鸭、斑头雁、鬃林鸭等50种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The common bronzewing (Phaps chalcoptera) is a species of medium-sized, heavily built pigeon. Native to Australia and one of the country's most common pigeons, the common bronzewing is able to live in almost any habitat, with the possible exception of very barren areas and dense rainforests. Its advertising call is an extraordinary mournful whooo repeated at metronomic intervals.{{Cite book |last=Pizzey |first=Graham |title=The Graham Pizzey & Frank Knight field guide to the birds of Australia |date=1999 |publisher=Angus & Robertson |others=Frank Knight |isbn=0-207-19691-5 |location=Pymble, N.S.W

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2024 |title=Treron phoenicopterus |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22691203A263632121 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22691203A263632121.en |access-date=20 December 2025}} | image = Yellow-footed green pigeon (Treron phoenicoptera) Photograph by Shantanu Kuveskar.jpg | image_caption = Pair in Mangaon, Maharashtra, India | genus = Treron | species = phoenicopterus | authority = (Latham, 1790) | synonyms = Treron phoenicoptera (Latham, 1790) | range_map = | range_map_caption = }} thumb|right|At Punjab Bhavan, New Delhi. The yellow-footed green pigeon (Treron phoenicopterus), also known as yellow-legged green pigeon, is a common species of green pigeon found in the Indian subcontinent and parts of Southeast Asia. It is the state bird of Maharashtra.{{cite news|author=Rebello, S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The yellow-crowned tyrannulet (Tyrannulus elatus) is a species of bird in subfamily Elaeniinae of family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Costa Rica, Panama, and in every mainland South American country except Argentina, Chile, Paraguay, and Uruguay.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The black-headed ibis (Threskiornis melanocephalus), also known as the Oriental white ibis, Indian white ibis, and black-necked ibis, is a species of wading bird of the ibis family Threskiornithidae which breeds in the South and Southeast Asia from India to the west and as far east as Japan. It is the only native ibis species in its range that has an overall white plumage with a black neck and head

birds.label: 分布于

links.evidence: The common bronzewing (Phaps chalcoptera) is a species of medium-sized, heavily built pigeon. Native to Australia and one of the country's most common pigeons, the common bronzewing is able to live in almost any habitat, with the possible exception of very barren areas and dense rainforests. Its advertising call is an extraordinary mournful whooo repeated at metronomic intervals.{{Cite book |last=Pizzey |first=Graham |title=The Graham Pizzey & Frank Knight field guide to the birds of Australia |date=1999 |publisher=Angus & Robertson |others=Frank Knight |isbn=0-207-19691-5 |location=Pymble, N.S.W

links.evidence: The black-headed ibis (Threskiornis melanocephalus), also known as the Oriental white ibis, Indian white ibis, and black-necked ibis, is a species of wading bird of the ibis family Threskiornithidae which breeds in the South and Southeast Asia from India to the west and as far east as Japan. It is the only native ibis species in its range that has an overall white plumage with a black neck and head
